raw_consumer_statement: After logging into my AMEX XXXX account ( XXXX ), I opened a chat session. Because of the poor customer service, I was going back and forth with customer service for almost XXXX hours. Afterwards, I was told that I had to speak with another department. I was transferred in the chat to another department. I was told that my complaint was not fraud but that I needed to speak with someone else. AGAIN, I was told the complaint wasn't fraud. So they transferred me. I had to begin the conversation again. Consequently, I made a purchase online. The customer service representative went into my account and placed a fraud alert on my account without my permission and without telling me. Then declined the transaction. They sent an email asking me to verify the transaction. I verified the transaction, and they still declined it. I went back to the chat and was told that transaction was approved. I then received a notice from the company that it was declined. I questioned this in the chat and was told that I must call. I called on XX/XX/year> and was told that the system is down and they can't talk to me. There is a saying in the XXXX community amongst the elders when something is really bad. It is called " XXXX poor ''. This was XXXX poor service. I asked that my purchases which needed to be completed at that time, were processed and now the " system is down and they can not help me ''. I had to ask XXXX  times for a supervisor. I was refused to have MY account released as I requested.
